author: codex

patch.value:
## Context

Project management is context management. JSONL is the lossless interchange format, so dropping context during persistence violates its core contract.

## Design

Use SDK-registered namespaced item fields for source metadata that cannot safely overwrite pm reserved audit fields, then map them back to stable public JSONL keys on export.
Preserve rich JSONL fields across installed pm-todos import/export
Greptile reproduced that JSONL parsing retained rich fields but runTodoImport dropped them before pm persistence, causing export-after-import data loss.

A real installed pm-todos package imports and exports id, title, description, status, type, priority, tags, deadline, assignee, sprint, source timestamps, todo dates, and kv without loss; object kv values normalize to JSON strings; package doctor reports zero warnings; unit and release gates pass.
PR #22 is checked out and Greptile/CodeRabbit findings are available.
Install the branch into an isolated tracker; import JSONL containing object kv and rich metadata; export JSONL; compare all public fields.
All supported JSONL context survives persistence and export.
Before this fix, kv, description, assignee, sprint, source timestamps, and todo dates were dropped or replaced.
Agents lose task context during backup, migration, or synchronization.

value.author: codex
value.text: Greptile review on PR #22 identified the persistence gap; CodeRabbit tracker findings were remediated in the same feedback round.

value.author: codex
value.text: Reserved pm audit fields must remain authoritative; source timestamps are preserved in namespaced SDK fields and projected back only at JSONL export.

value.author: codex
value.text: Parser-level round-trip tests are insufficient for importers: persistence and re-export must be tested through a real installed package.

message: Track JSONL persistence fidelity review fix

patch.value: Real isolated install/import/export comparison passed for all 15 fields; package doctor reported zero warnings and five item fields; 119 tests and the full release gate passed with zero vulnerabilities.
Installed-package JSONL import/export preserves all 15 supported public fields without mutating pm-owned audit metadata.

Registered five namespaced SDK item fields, persisted every rich JSONL field through create/update, projected source metadata back to stable public keys, preserved source IDs, and normalized object kv values to compact JSON strings.
Rich JSONL persistence is lossless through the real installed package; unit, doctor, audit, package, and changelog gates pass.

A real isolated pm CLI 2026.7.10 install/import/export comparison passed for all 15 JSONL fields; automated installed-package integration coverage passes against the declared compatibility floor; package doctor reported zero warnings and five item fields; 120 tests and the full release gate pass with zero vulnerabilities.

120 tests plus installed-package JSONL import/export/sync pass; real --file sync works; restrictive-filter sync returns a USAGE error and preserves the source bytes; production audit is clean; package/changelog gates pass; no dist artifacts remain tracked.

Preserved rich JSONL fields through declared SDK fields, made sync inputs fully declared, rejected empty filter values, and added a default no-empty-overwrite guard with explicit --allow-empty acknowledgement.

value.author: codex
value.text: Bidirectional sync must treat a zero-row export as potentially destructive context loss; require explicit acknowledgement before replacing a non-empty source file.

value.author: codex
value.text: Review hardening: treat blank JSONL source-date metadata as absent before constructing --field arguments, preventing malformed empty date values from reaching the core CLI.
